title: Module 1
content:
Occupational safety and industrial safety, Human and organizational factors
approach to industrial safety, Definition and scope of human and
organizational factors (HOF) in safety. Historical development of HOF, rule-
based safety, managed safety, definition and model of safety culture,
Elements of the safety culture model, Organisational behaviour, Safety
outcomes,

title: Module 2
content:
Human Error Types and Models: Types of human errors, Causes and
consequences, Errors and Violations, Models of human error (Swiss Cheese
Model, SHEL Model). Techniques for human error identification., Error
producing Conditions, Human error in system design and operations.
Techniques for managing and mitigating human error. Error-tolerant design
and resilient systems. Case studies on human error in engineering incidents
(e.g., Chernobyl, Deepwater Horizon). Principles of ergonomics in
engineering design (physical, cognitive, organizational ergonomics). Human-
machine interface design, usability, and accessibility.

title: Module 3
content:
Safety Management Systems (SMS), Components and principles of SMS
(policy, risk management, assurance, promotion). Integration of human
factors into SMS. Behavior-Based Safety (BBS), Principles of BBS and its
implementation in engineering environments, Role of observation, feedback,
and reinforcement in safety improvement, Case studies on BBS applications.,
Training and Competency Management, Role of simulation, drills, and
scenario-based training. Overview of key safety standards (ISO 45001, ISO
31000)

title: Module 4
content:
Human Reliability Analysis (HRA), Overview of HRA methodologies
(THERP, HEART, SPAR-H), Application of HRA in complex systems and
high-risk industries., Evaluation and mitigation of human errors through
HRA.
Automation and human factors challenges (AI, robotics, autonomous
systems)., Digitalization and safety management (wearables, digital twins,
big data analytics). Balancing productivity, cost, and safety
